acceptance.assertion: Workspace and task store derive the same digest from one canonical argv serialization.
Any argv or serialization drift is detected before writer execution.
Existing receipts are handled through an explicit versioned compatibility policy.

goal: Use one canonical argv_sha256 algorithm across Workspace, durable task store and receipts.

does_not_establish: identity equivalence for non-argv command representations
permission to retry ambiguous historical task starts
metadata.reason: PR #193 plus successful formal workspace role starts resolved the Workspace and Task Store argv digest mismatch.

validation.github_ci: required validate (3.10) and validate (3.12) jobs passed on all cited implementation PRs

validated_acceptance.canonical-digest: PR #193 routes Workspace and durable task identity through the shared canonical argv serialization.
validated_acceptance.drift-blocked: The formal workspace writer passed preflight and started without the former argv_sha256 mismatch; regression tests reject digest drift before execution.
validated_acceptance.migration: PR #193 carries an explicit versioned command-identity compatibility contract rather than silently reinterpreting historical receipts.

metadata.verification_note: PR #193 unified argv digest binding; the formal workspace preflight and role start completed without the previous Workspace/Task Store identity mismatch, and required CI passed.

title: Unify Agent Workspace argv digest binding
